rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring John Cahill, Jr., And Archer Norris Aplc To Pay The Costs And Fees For The Failure To Appear At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for an order requiring John Cahill, Jr. and Archer Norris to pay the costs and fees for the failure to appear at deposition: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PERNIZ VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Fdcc California, Inc. To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, February 6, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant FDCC California, Inc. to produce custodian(s) of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is denied. Defendant, a dissolved corporation, is under no obligation to produce a deponent. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:00a.m. in Department 611, Hon. Lynn O¿Malley-Taylor presiding. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (611/LOT)

(LOT/611)
